title: lepidagathis pavala
intro: Lepidagathis pavala, also known as Pavala in Ayurvedic medicine, is a plant species belonging to the family Acanthaceae, which is native to the tropical regions of India and Sri Lanka. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, to treat various health conditions such as fever, skin disorders, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lepidagathis pavala include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or an infusion by boiling its leaves in water, which is then consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
